database_connectivity.code:
from sqlalchemy import create_engine from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, declarative_base
from app.core.config import DATABASE_URL
engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL) SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine) Base = declarative_base()
# Inject session dependency into routes def get_db(): db = SessionLocal() try: yield db finally: db.close()
database_connectivity.instructions: Use this SAME EXACT structure in Flask: - No async - No advanced connection pools - No external ORMs - Only SQLAlchemy core ORM with sessionmaker - Each repository receives the DB session

layers.layer: Main App (only initializes and registers blueprints)
Routers (handle API endpoints, use schemas for validation)
Models Layer (SQLAlchemy models matching DB fields exactly)
Schemas Layer (Pydantic or Marshmallow request/response DTOs)

folder_structure.main_file: main.py (initialize app, register blueprints, load database)

database_models.description: Create SQLAlchemy models with EXACT field names from database:

requirements.rule: Routers must NOT contain business logic.
Service layer must NOT contain SQL.
Repository layer must contain all SQLAlchemy CRUD operations.
Schemas must handle validation and serialization.
Models must strictly match the DB schema.

deliverables.item: Database connection module using your SQLAlchemy style
Sample CRUD implementation for all layers
